State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660      322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444

February 5, 1981


Kelly Meagher
Butte Creek Canyon
Citizens Committee
Post Office Box 1892
Chico, CA 95927

A-81-03 -005

Dear Ms. Meagher:

Thank you for your letter asking whether or not the
Butte County Board of Supervisors is disqualified from
making decisions which affect campaign contributors.

The Political Reform Act of 1974 specifically exempts
campaign contributions from the definition of income so
that there is no disqualification under the Act when
contributions are received. (See Section 82930(b)(1) of
the enclosed Act.)

Last year we studied the question of contributions
influencing governmental decisions and issued a report
which I have enclosed.

I would be interested in hearing from you if you
have any comments about the report.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:inc

Enclosures

Butte Creek Canyon Citizens Committee
P.O. Box 1892
Chico, CA 95927 — 916/342-8551

December 31, 1980

Fair Political Practice Commission
1100 K St. Bldg.
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear General Council,

We of the Butte Creek Canyon Citizens Committee are
a concerned group of landowners and residents in Butte
County. We have organized in opposition to a proposed
PA-C development in Butte Creek Canyon. This development
is proposed by Butte Creek Rock Co. which is owned by
Baldwin Contracting Co. of Marysville, CA., a subsidiary
of M & T Inc. of San Francisco.

On Dec. 16, 1980, the Butte County Board of Super-
visors voted 4 to 1 to approve a motion of intent to
approve this PA-C project. It has recently come to our
attention that 3 of the 4 Board members who voted to
approve this motion have recieved substantial campaign
contributions in the last year from the proponents of
this project. These contributions have come either
directly from Butte Creek Rock Co., Baldwin Contracting
Co., M & T Inc. and Dallas Lewis (President - Butte
Creek Rock Co.), or indirectly, through a political
action fund, the Butte County Political Action Committee.

The question has been raised that there may be a
conflict of interest here. We would appreciate an
official ruling from you as to whether or not these
supervisors should abstain from voting on this matter.
The Board will be meeting again on Jan. 6, 1981 to vote
their formal approval of this project.

We have attached copies of several campaign con-
tribution forms from Butte County Supervisors Robert
Winston, Bertha Moseley, Robert Lemke and from the
Butte County Political Action Committee.

Sincerely,
Bonnie Burnham
Kelly Meagher
Teresa Kludt

Butte Creek Canyon
Citizens Committee

2. Amount received less than $100 (Not itemized): $25
3. Total monetary contributions this period (Line 1 + Line 2): $625

This document is a campaign statement form 420, filed on July 29, 1980, for the Butte County Political Action Committee. The statement covers the period from May 20, 1980, through August 30, 1980. The committee's address is 341 Broadway, P.O. Box 4226, Chico, CA 95927, and the treasurer's address is 17 Yale Way, Chico, CA 95926.

The form lists expenditures by candidates or officeholders and measures, with the following entries:

- ROBERT WINSTON assembly candidate: zx (support), $600, cumulative to date $2,000.
- Superior Court Judge Loyd Mulkey-Candidate: xx (support), $300, cumulative to date $300.
- Wally Herger-Assembly candidate: xx (support), $27.45, cumulative to date $27.45.

The verification section states that the statement and its schedules are true, correct, and complete, and that all reasonable diligence was used in their preparation. The signature of the treasurer, F.L. Willis, Jr., is present.

This document is a financial contribution receipt form, specifically Form 420, 430 or 490, used for monetary contributions received by a political committee. The form is titled "Monetary Contributions Received" and indicates that amounts may be rounded to whole dollars.

The statement covers the period from May 20, 1980, through June 30, 1980, with an I.D. number of 801692.

The name of the candidate or committee is listed as "Butte County Political Action Committee."

There is one entry on the form:

- **Date Rec'd**: 5-28-80
- **Full Name and Address of Contributor**: M & T Inc., 369 Pine Street, San Francisco 94104
- **Occupation**: Corporation engaged in farming etc.
- **Employer**: Same
- **Amount Received**: $500.00
- **Cumulative to Date**: $500.00

If more space is needed, there is an option to check a box and attach additional Schedules A.

**Summary:**

1. Amount received, $100 or more (Include all Schedule A subtotals): $500.00
2. Amount received less than $100 (Not itemized): $50.00
3. Total Monetary Contributions This Period (Line 1 + Line 2): $550.00

This document is an official financial report form, specifically Form 561 from 1980, used by entities that have made expenditures or contributions totaling $5,000 or more in a calendar year directly to or at the behest of a candidate, officer, or committee; or persons who have made independent expenditures on behalf of a candidate, officer, or ballot measure in the amount of $500 or more.

embedding.first_500_words: February 5, 1981 Kelly Meagher Butte Creek Canyon Citizens Committee Post Office Box 1892 Chico, CA 95927 Dear Ms. Meagher: Thank you for your letter asking whether or not the Butte County Board of Supervisors is disqualified from making decisions which affect campaign contributors. The Political Reform Act of 1974 specifically exempts campaign contributions from the definition of income so that there is no disqualification under the Act when contributions are received. (See Section 82030(b)(1) of the enclosed Act.) Last year we studied the question of contributions influencing governmental decisions and issued a report which I have enclosed. I would be interested in hearing from you if you have any comments about the report. Sincerely, [signature] Robert M. Stern General Counsel RMS:nc Enclosures December 31, 1980 Fair Political Practice Commission 1100 K St. Bldg. Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear General Council, We of the Butte Creek Canyon Citizens Committee are a concerned group of landowners and residents in Butte County. We have organized in opposition to a proposed PA-C development in Butte Creek Canyon. This development is proposed by Butte Creek Rock Co. which is owned by Baldwin Contracting Co. of Marysville, Ca., a subsidiary of M & T Inc. of San Francisco. On Dec. 16, 1980, the Butte County Board of Supervisors voted 4 to 1 to approve a motion of intent to approve this PA-C project. It has recently come to our attention that 3 of the 4 Board members who voted to approve this motion have received substantial campaign contributions in the last year from the proponents of this project. These contributions have come either directly from Butte Creek Rock Co., Baldwin Contracting Co., M & T Inc. and Dallas Lewis (President - Butte Creek Rock Co.), or indirectly, through a political action fund, the Butte County Political Action Committee. The question has been raised that there may be a conflict of interest here. We would appreciate an official ruling from you as to whether or not these supervisors should abstain from voting on this matter. The Board will be meeting again on Jan. 6, 1981 to vote their formal approval of this project. We have attached copies of several campaign contribution forms from Butte County Supervisors Robert Winston, Bertha Moseley, Robert Lemke and from the Butte County Political Action Committee. Sincerely, Bonnie Burnham Kelly Meagher Teresa Kludt Butte Creek Canyon Citizens Committee This document is a financial report titled "Monetary Contributions Received" for the period from 4-22-80 to 5-19-80, as indicated on the form. The report lists contributions received by the Butte County Political Action Committee and includes the following details: **NAME OF CANDIDATE OR COMMITTEE:** Butte County Political Action Committee **STATEMENT COVERS PERIOD:** From 4-22-80 Through 5-19-80 **I.D. NUMBER (IF COMMITTEE):** 807692 **DATE REC'D | FULL NAME AND ADDRESS OF CONTRIBUTOR | OCCUPATION | EMPLOYER | AMOUNT RECEIVED | CUMULATIVE TO DATE** | 980-22- | DRAKE HOMES | Contractor | Corporation | $1,000. | $1,000. | | -22- | BUTTE CREEK ROCK CO. Skyway Chico, California
